keyBranches.feat/tui-json-render-surface: ce4a8bc (3 commits ahead of main, not merged)

codexReviewBranch.name: codex/review-branch-for-merge-into-main
keyChanges: agent.ts: removed ~128 lines, imports cleaned up (channel-specific code removed)
shared/types.ts: removed TelegramStickerInputMetadata, TelegramPhotoInputMetadata, TaskInputPlatformMetadata, MonoSensitiveActionMode, MonoSandboxMode, MonoApprovalPolicy types
autonomy-runtime.ts: kept but detangled from agent.ts imports

observations: fix/stale-lockfile and feat/tui-json-render-surface are separate branches, fix/stale-lockfile is merged ahead of main
feat/tui-json-render-surface refactors Telegram-specific permission logic into generic ChannelPermissionProfile
streamAiSdkText function adds lower-level text streaming capability to LLM adapters
transcript-repair.ts provides model-specific sanitization for session history repair
HeartbeatWakeController implements priority-based coalescing for heartbeat triggers
ChannelRegistry creates surface+integration layer for multi-channel architecture
TUI now hosts Telegram integration as a pluggable component via ChannelIntegration
MonoTuiChannelConfig adds specMode (deterministic/generative) config for TUI rendering
codex review-branch is a major cleanup removing 25k+ lines, mostly Telegram-specific hardcoding
Mono's prompt system uses Nunjucks with 35 categorized template IDs (agent/ui/memory)
UI waiting templates are structure-identical parameterized by emoji/prefix/action/suffix variables
Memory templates include compacted_step_* series and backend-specific context blocks (openviking/seekdb)
autonomy_extra_context.j2 has structured risk/priority/intent context with curiosity_probe special format
